title: The Illusion of Multitasking
author: CET6 Reading
content: In our modern, hyper-connected world, multitasking is often worn as a badge of honor. We pride ourselves on answering emails while listening to a podcast and scrolling through social media. However, neuroscientists warn that the human brain is not wired for simultaneous complex tasks. What we perceive as multitasking is actually task-switching, a process that drains cognitive resources and reduces overall efficiency. True deep work requires singular focus, a state of flow where distractions are silenced and the mind can engage profoundly with a single subject.
